etymology.description: semantic 心 (heart) + phonetic 白 (bái) -> fear
etymology.interpretation: The character 怕 (pà, "fear") combines the semantic component 心 (xīn, "heart") with the phonetic component 白 (bái). The heart component implies an emotional state or feeling, and when combined with 白, which provides the sound, it suggests the feeling of fear or being afraid. The heart as the center of emotions is intuitively associated with fear.

examples.english: He is afraid of the dark.

examples.chinese: 妈妈怕我们饿了(媽媽怕我們餓了)，给我们准备了很多食物(給我們準備了很多食物)。
examples.pinyin: Māmā pà wǒmen è le, gěi wǒmen zhǔnbèi le hěn duō shíwù.
examples.english: Mom is afraid we might be hungry, so she prepared a lot of food for us.

examples.chinese: 小孩子们怕打针(小孩子們怕打針)，所以医生很温柔(所以醫生很溫柔)。
examples.pinyin: Xiǎo háizi men pà dǎzhēn, suǒyǐ yīshēng hěn wēnróu.
examples.english: Children are afraid of injections, so the doctor is very gentle.
